'''	Matthew Hugh Calland''' (born 20 August 1971) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, and [[coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] (two spells), [[Featherstone Rovers]], the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Hull FC]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]], as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}}, and coached at club level for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].

==Background==
Matt Calland was born in [[Widnes]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Rochdale Hornets===
Calland started his professional [[rugby league]] career with [[Rochdale Hornets]], signing from Oldham St Annes in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |title=Calland forced to retire through injury |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/other-sport/calland-forced-to-retire-through-injury-1139184 |website=Manchester Evening News |access-date=2 January 2024}}</ref> He played in Rochdale Hornets 14–24 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1991 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]], on Sunday 20 October 1991. He joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] in September 1993 for a transfer fee of £30,000.<ref>{{cite news |title=Wales receive World Cup call |work=The Guardian |date=28 September 1993 |location=London |page=19 |id={{ProQuest|187470300}}}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
In November 1995, Calland was signed by [[Bradford Bulls]] from [[Featherstone Rovers]] as part of an exchange deal which saw [[Deryck Fox]] and [[Roy Powell (rugby league, born 1965)|Roy Powell]] transfer from Bradford to Featherstone.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan join the hunt for Newlove |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/wigan-join-the-hunt-for-newlove-1582087.html |website=The Independent |access-date=2 January 2024 |date=15 November 1995}}</ref>

Calland played at {{rlp|ce}} in Bradford Bulls' 32–40 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 26 April 1996.<ref name="steveprescottfoundation.co.uk">{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> and was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in Bradford Bulls' 22–32 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1997 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 3 May 1997.<ref name="steveprescottfoundation.co.uk"/>

===International honours===
Matt Calland won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 1996 against France (sub).

==Coaching career==
Calland began coaching at Hopwood Hall College in 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Interview with Matt Calland |url=https://www.realrochdale.co.uk/editions/autumn-2019/interview-with-matt-calland/ |website=Real Rochdale |access-date=3 January 2024}}</ref> He was named the assistant coach at Halifax in June 2006. [[Martin Hall (rugby league)|Martin Hall]] moved upstairs in October to take up the post of director of football. He was then named the new head coach of [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. In 2010, he coached the team to victory in the Championship Grand Final against favourites [[Featherstone Rovers]]. They came back from 4–22 down to win 23–22 in extra time. It was the first trophy Halifax had won in 23 years.<ref>{{cite news|title=Calland happy with options|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/local/Calland-happy-with-options.4558865.jp|work=Halifax Evening Courier|date=4 October 2008|accessdate=16 October 2008}}</ref>

In May 2019, he was named as head coach at [[Rochdale Hornets]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Rochdale Hornets name Matt Calland as head coach |url=https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/127647/rugby-league-rochdale-hornets-name-matt-calland-as-head-coach |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240103195948/https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/127647/rugby-league-rochdale-hornets-name-matt-calland-as-head-coach |url-status=usurped |archive-date=3 January 2024 |website=Rochdale Online |access-date=3 January 2024 |date=3 May 2019}}</ref> He left the club by mutual consent in August 2022.<ref>{{cite web |title=Head coach Matt Calland leaves Rochdale Hornets |url=https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/147323/head-coach-matt-calland-leaves-rochdale-hornets |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220815170358/https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/147323/head-coach-matt-calland-leaves-rochdale-hornets |url-status=usurped |archive-date=15 August 2022 |website=Rochdale Online |access-date=3 January 2024 |date=1 August 2022}}</ref>
